opp.name: Cooke [iSource & P2P]
opp.next_step: March 5 - Hi Suvro, Cassio, Thank you for the update — I appreciate you keeping me in the loop. I completely understand that the core team needs time to align on the next course of action. If it would be helpful, I’m happy to provide any additional information or connect with stakeholders to support the decision-making process. In the meantime, do you have a sense of the expected timeline for when next steps might be determined? That will help us plan resources accordingly. Looking forward to hearing from you.
opp.ais_why:
• Products include Merlin and Merlin Intake, and the Merlin product line confirms it. AI importance is rated 'Neutral' and the opportunity info lists interest in P2P and S2C. The next step is a contract holdup while the buyer waits for internal alignment. Four Avoma meetings sit behind this, including a 96-minute Cooke + Tassal joint session on November 20 where AI features were demoed at length.
• On the November 20 call, Chris Ellis from Tassal expressed excitement about committing to procurement automation and attendees called the RFP question generator "cool". Buyers asked about AI-powered supplier onboarding (W-8/W-9 OCR). But the bulk of the buyer dialogue stayed on Business Central integration, freight CSV coding and goods receipt - operational, not AI-led.
• Cooke also discussed CLM with AI Instareview, but it was driven by the vendor demo flow. Tassal is on a separate account, and Cooke's own next-step says the deal is stalled while the core team aligns internally. AI features were discussed, but the buyer voice is operationally pragmatic rather than AI-mandated.

contacts.name: Lindsay MacDonald
contacts.title: Manager, IT Security - Governance, Risk and Compliance (GRC)

items.quote: Pricing deferred until a system is selected; no CFO/CIO/EB engaged.
items.source: Avoma demo cycle Oct-Dec 2025 + SF contact roles

items.quote: We will communicate the next steps when there are credible next steps in action.
General ROI described as ever-evolving; no savings figure shared.

metrics.narrative: Cooke articulated real qualitative pain but never quantified it. On the 28 Mar 2025 discovery call the project lead framed the goal as leveraging a 5.5B dollar company's scale to consolidate fragmented, plant-by-plant buying into global spend leverage, and the Chile/Seajoy demo (08 Dec 2025) surfaced manual, email-driven ordering and no supplier portal. No dollar savings target, headcount, or cycle-time number was ever put on the table, and the general ROI the buyer referenced earlier was described as ever-evolving and never shared. The move is a joint ROI workshop anchored to Cooke's own spend once the relationship is reopened.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (Christopher Dever, Manager of Project Management, PRIMARY, Evaluator)
champion.narrative: Christopher Dever (Manager of Project Management, the primary Salesforce contact and Evaluator) is a strong coach but not yet a true champion, and his engagement has lapsed. He coordinated the multi-region demo cycle through Dec 2025 and holds clear internal process access, but he never introduced Zycus to an Economic Buyer, never shared competitive intelligence, and never confirmed Zycus as the preferred vendor. He has been silent through the 2026 stall, and the only 2026 buyer reply came from Suvraprateem Burman, not Dever. What has developed is process access and platform familiarity; what is missing is advocacy, a power introduction, and any post-demo urgency. Re-securing him (or replacing him with an engaged procurement sponsor) is prerequisite to any commercial step.

competition.narrative: Cooke is evaluating Zycus against a Gartner-quadrant shortlist recorded in Salesforce Competitors__c: Coupa, GEP Worldwide, iValua, and SAP Ariba. GEP remains the strongest peer threat given Cooke's multi-entity global footprint, with Coupa and Ariba the familiar enterprise brands and iValua the wildcard. Critically, no competitor has visibly advanced to commercials or contracting, and the 2026 silence reflects an internal pause across the whole evaluation rather than a Zycus-specific loss, so this is a stall to break, not a bake-off to win right now. There is no new 2026 competitive evidence this sweep, so the field and threat levels are carried forward unchanged. The wedge remains Merlin adoption ease plus flexible multi-ERP deployment.

identify_pain.narrative: The operational pain is clear and repeatedly stated. Cooke runs a fragmented, multi-entity, multi-ERP procurement estate (Great Plains, Navision/Business Central, and SAP across Canada, Chile and Honduras) with manual, email-driven ordering, no supplier portal, weak spend visibility, and maverick buying at plant level. On the 08 Dec 2025 Chile/Seajoy call the IT team (a Seajoy IT manager and application-development staff) confirmed the pain from the technology side and pressed hard on how Zycus integrates with their varied regional ERPs. The pain owner is the IT project-management office plus regional procurement leadership; the financial impact remains unquantified.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (Lindsay MacDonald, IT Security GRC)
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are entirely unstarted. There has been no MSA/SOW discussion, no redlining, and no procurement channel defined. InfoSec is the one concrete thread: Lindsay MacDonald (Manager, IT Security - Governance, Risk and Compliance) is a Salesforce contact and the rep offered SOC 2 reports on 30 Mar 2026, but no security review has been scheduled. Implementation was floated as an EY-led Latam delivery on the 08 Dec 2025 call, which hints at a systems-integrator role but nothing formal. A confirmed timeline and a first InfoSec session would create the paper-process visibility that is missing today.

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer is engaged or confirmed. The Salesforce contact roles carry Julio RamosAguirre (Vice President, Procurement) as the most senior procurement stakeholder and the logical budget owner, but he has no assigned role on the opportunity and has only appeared as a demo attendee, never in a commercial or pricing conversation. No CFO, CIO, or CEO is named in any contact role, and the project lead deferred all pricing talk during the demo cycle. Mapping Julio RamosAguirre (or the finance/IT budget owner above him) and confirming who signs is the gating move before the deal can progress.

decision_process.narrative: The process the buyer is actually running is opaque and currently paused. The call sequence shows a broad, multi-region evaluation (North America, Spain, Chile/Seajoy, plus a joint Tassal session) coordinated by the IT project-management office through late 2025, but no shortlist decision, no approval chain, and no procurement/legal path has been shared. The buyer's own words on 25 Mar 2026 (status-quo, will communicate when there are credible next steps) confirm the go/no-go sits with an internal core team the deal team cannot see. Getting the buyer to name who owns the decision and the timeline is the primary unknown to close.

decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer's criteria are inferred from the demo agenda rather than a documented scorecard. Across the Chile/Seajoy session (08 Dec 2025) the focus was ease of adoption (Merlin Intake inside Microsoft Teams so occasional users are not retrained), multi-ERP integration (Great Plains, Navision/Business Central, and SAP across Cooke's regional entities), supplier onboarding, sourcing, CLM with Instareview, and P2P with two- and three-way matching. These map to Cooke's stated need for one configurable platform across fragmented entities, but no weighted requirements document or written evaluation criteria has been confirmed. Securing the buyer's BRD or requirements matrix would convert this from inferred to confirmed.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Losing on capability fit — AIS is 5.0 / Moderate. The calls corroborate this: AI features were shown and the RFP generator was called cool, but the buyer voice is operationally pragmatic and integration-first, not AI-mandated. Fairly rated, not under-rated.
bullets.text: Losing on capability fit — AIS is 5.0 / Moderate.

bullets.full: No real champion yet — Christopher Dever coordinated the full demo cycle and holds real internal process access, but he never advocated for Zycus over rivals, never opened a path to the Economic Buyer, and has been silent through the 2026 pause. That makes him a coach, not a committed champion, and he is at risk of being disempowered or the project shelved. The path forward is to re-engage him and, in parallel, build a second sponsor in procurement leadership.

blocks.read: Lukewarm — some motion, but not strong.
blocks.title: Deal momentum

bullets.text: Live next steps with named dates

blocks.title: What moves it forward

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 17 is driven by Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 28 is carried by competitive strength +0.20 (weight 15); business case strength +0.30 (weight 10). Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 54 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 1 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 12 is anchored on the stage win (28), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 54, and dragged by risk 17. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 1 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS is 5.0 / Moderate. The calls corroborate this: AI features were shown and the RFP generator was called cool, but the buyer voice is operationally pragmatic and integration-first, not AI-mandated. Fairly rated, not under-rated.

items.change: Buyer confirmed the project is on hold pending internal alignment
Ownership transferred from Edward Dlugosz to Mike Flowers
Close date pushed from 29 May 2026 to 18 Sep 2026 by the deal owner

deal_movement.summary: No forward buyer movement in 2026. The last substantive buyer signal (25 Mar 2026) was an explicit pause; the only changes since are internal Salesforce edits (owner reassignment to Mike Flowers on 03 Apr 2026, close date pushed to 18 Sep 2026 on 14 May 2026, division re-tag on 26 May 2026). The deal has been coasting since the Oct-Dec 2025 demo cycle.

items.action: Mike Flowers emails Christopher Dever to reintroduce himself and ask the internal-alignment timeline.
items.trigger: New owner since 03 Apr 2026 gives a clean reason to reset the relationship after the 25 Mar pause.
items.expected_effect: Reopens a dark thread and surfaces whether the project is live or shelved; if no reply in 10 business days, escalate to procurement leadership.

items.expected_effect: Builds a second, more senior sponsor and a path to budget authority the champion never opened.

items: No buyer call or meeting in 2026
Rep outreach 05 and 24 Mar 2026 produced no forward step
confidence_signals.summary: Buyer engagement is dark. No 2026 calls, the only 2026 reply was an explicit pause, and the champion has gone quiet. Momentum has cooled to a stall.

north_star_verdict.math: Created and qualified 28 Mar 2025. Stage Formal Evaluation since Sep 2025 (about 9 months in stage). Last buyer touch 30 Mar 2026 (~95 days ago); last substantive buyer reply 25 Mar 2026 stating status-quo. Days to close 77. Remaining path (EB validation, commercials, ROI, MSA/SOW, InfoSec, contract) cannot complete in 77 days from a silent, paused buyer, so the close date is indefensible.
evidence: Salesforce LastActivityDate 30 Mar 2026, ~95 days silent as of 03 Jul 2026
Buyer email 25 Mar 2026 (Suvraprateem Burman): 'There is currently status-quo on the project next steps. We will communicate the next steps when there are credible next steps in action'
Rep outreach 05 Mar and 24 Mar 2026 asking for a timeline; only the status-quo reply came back
Stage Formal Evaluation entered Sep 2025, no progression to Shortlisted or Vendor Selected
No calls recorded in 2026; last demo cycle ran Oct-Dec 2025
Ownership changed to Mike Flowers 03 Apr 2026; new owner has not yet re-established the relationship
north_star_verdict.headline: Buyer paused the project and has gone quiet for roughly 95 days; stuck in Formal Evaluation about 9 months with no Economic Buyer, no commercials, and a Sep 18 close that is not achievable from a cold restart.

flags: New owner Mike Flowers should use the ownership change as a clean reason to reopen the relationship, not chase the silent prior thread.
Economic Buyer never mapped; multi-thread to Julio RamosAguirre (VP Procurement) to reach budget authority before the window closes.
Champion Christopher Dever is an Evaluator with access but no advocacy and has gone quiet; re-qualify whether the project is paused or he is disempowered.
best_practice_check.summary: Cold, paused deal with a broad evaluation but no power access; the winning levers are a fresh-owner reset, mapping the budget owner, and re-qualifying whether the project is live.

competitive_position.summary: Cooke is weighing Zycus against a Gartner-quadrant shortlist: GEP Worldwide, Coupa, SAP Ariba, and iValua (per Salesforce Competitors__c, named at the 28 Mar 2025 discovery). GEP is the strongest current threat as the closest peer for Cooke's multi-entity global rollout; Coupa and Ariba are the incumbent brand options; iValua is the wildcard. No competitor has advanced to commercials, and the 95-day 2026 silence points to an internal pause across all vendors rather than a Zycus loss. No new 2026 evidence this run, so threat levels are held as carried forward.

competitors.name: GEP Worldwide
competitors.quote: We reviewed the Gartner S2P report and shortlisted vendors from the quadrant; Zycus is one of them.
competitors.how_we_win: Position Merlin adoption ease and flexible multi-entity workflows against GEP enterprise rigidity; pilot one entity fast to prove rollout speed.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: We are talking to a few obvious players, Coupa is one of them.
competitors.how_we_win: Frame Zycus as equal functionality at lower TCO with faster time to value; lead with Heineken multi-country, multi-ERP success.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: SAP Ariba is on the shortlist from the Gartner report.
competitors.how_we_win: Contrast platform-agnostic multi-ERP integration and stronger Merlin AI against Ariba lock-in and dated UX for Cooke's non-SAP entities.

competitors.name: iValua
competitors.quote: iValua is part of the Gartner shortlist we are considering.
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with P2P adoption and Merlin Intake where iValua is weaker, matching Cooke's stated P2P and supplier-management priority.

items.deliverable: Communicate the internal-alignment decision and the timeline for next steps
items.grounding_quote: There is currently status-quo on the project next steps. We will communicate the next steps when there are credible next steps in action.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Merlin and Merlin Intake were demoed at length (RFP question generation, Instareview CLM, guided buying, invoice matching) and drew mild interest, but Cooke's dialogue stayed operational (integration, goods receipt, freight coding) rather than AI-led. The agentic story is present but not the buyer's driver.

items.quote: Cooke has many ERPs: Great Plains and Navision/Business Central in Canada, SAP in Honduras.
items.criterion: One platform across fragmented multi-ERP entities

items.quote: It lives inside Microsoft Teams, so there is no need to learn a new platform.
items.criterion: Ease of adoption for occasional users

items.quote: Pricing and ROI were never discussed with the budget owner.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Cooke expects one configurable platform that unifies fragmented, multi-ERP entities with easy end-user adoption. Zycus demonstrated strong fit on adoption and integration flexibility, but fit on commercials, security, and decision timeline is unproven because the deal stalled before those stages.

Four Avoma meetings sit behind this, including a 96-minute Cooke + Tassal joint session on November 20 where AI features were demoed at length.

But the bulk of the buyer dialogue stayed on Business Central integration, freight CSV coding and goods receipt - operational, not AI-led.

hard.next_step:
March 5 - Hi Suvro, Cassio,

 

Thank you for the update — I appreciate you keeping me in the loop.

 

I completely understand that the core team needs time to align on the next course of action. If it would be helpful, I’m happy to provide any additional information or connect with stakeholders to support the decision-making process.

 

In the meantime, do you have a sense of the expected timeline for when next steps might be determined? That will help us plan resources accordingly.

 

 

Looking forward to hearing from you.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain (fragmented multi-entity buying, no spend visibility, manual ordering) is clear, but ROI was described as ever-evolving and no savings figure was quantified.

gaps: Zero buyer calls in 2026; current-state read is inferred from the 2025 demo cycle plus 2026 email activity.
Economic Buyer identity and budget ownership genuinely unknown across all sources.
Decision process, shortlist status, and timeline not shared by the buyer.
Nov 20 2025 session was a joint Cooke/Tassal demo and the Dec 8 attendees were the Seajoy (Chile) entity; none match Salesforce Cooke contact roles, so they are not promoted as named stakeholders.

calls_omitted.reason: no transcript available this run; content carried from prior sweep

evidence_coverage.salesforce_window: activities 120d; field history to 2025-10; contact roles full

customer_preference.evidence: Buyer went quiet and stated status-quo on 25 Mar 2026; no Zycus-specific next step requested, dialogue stayed neutral and operational.
